root_text.ud2.9:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati pubbārāme migāramātupāsāde.
root_text.ud2.9:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena visākhāya migāramātuyā kocideva attho raññe pasenadimhi kosale paṭibaddho hoti.
root_text.ud2.9:1.4: Taṁ rājā pasenadi kosalo na yathādhippāyaṁ tīreti.
root_text.ud2.9:2.1: Atha kho visākhā migāramātā divā divassa yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.ud2.9:2.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho visākhaṁ migāramātaraṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.9:2.3: “handa kuto nu tvaṁ, visākhe, āgacchasi divā divassā”ti?
root_text.ud2.9:2.4: “Idha me, bhante, kocideva attho raññe pasenadimhi kosale paṭibaddho;
root_text.ud2.9:2.5: taṁ rājā pasenadi kosalo na yathādhippāyaṁ tīretī”ti.
root_text.ud2.9:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud2.9:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in the stilt longhouse of Migāra’s mother in the Eastern Monastery.
translation_text.ud2.9:1.3: Now at that time Visākhā was caught up in some business with King Pasenadi.
translation_text.ud2.9:1.4: But the king’s settlement did not meet her expectations.
translation_text.ud2.9:2.1: Then, in the middle of the day, she went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down.
translation_text.ud2.9:2.3: “So, Visākhā, where are you coming from in the middle of the day?”
translation_text.ud2.9:2.4: “Sir, I am caught up in some business with King Pasenadi.
translation_text.ud2.9:2.5: But the king’s settlement did not meet my expectations.”
translation_text.ud2.9:3.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud2.9:4.2: all under one’s own authority is pleasing;
translation_text.ud2.9:4.3: what’s shared is stressful for both parties,
translation_text.ud2.9:4.4: for yokes are hard to escape.”

variant_text.ud2.9:2.1: migāramātā divā divassa → divādivasseva (sya-all); divādivase yeva (pts-vp-pli1); divā divassayeva (mr)
